title: Kheer Bhawani
tagline: Kheer Bhawani — spiritual town of Jammu & Kashmir

overview.short: Kheer Bhawani, Ksheer Bhawani or the Ragnya Devi temple is a Hindu temple situated at a distance of 25 kilometres (16 mi) north-east of Srinagar, Jammu and Kashmir, India, in the v
overview.description: Kheer Bhawani, Ksheer Bhawani or the Ragnya Devi temple is a Hindu temple situated at a distance of 25 kilometres (16 mi) north-east of Srinagar, Jammu and Kashmir, India, in the village of Tulmulla in Ganderbal. It is dedicated to the Hindu goddess Kheer Bhavani constructed over a sacred spring. As is the custom with Hindu deities, the goddess has many names including Ragnya or Rajna, along with variations in honorifics such as Devi, Mata or Bhagavati. The term kheer refers to a milk and rice pudding that is offered to propitiate the goddess.

topPlaces.name: 1998 Wandhama massacre
topPlaces.description: The 1998 Wandhama massacre refers to the killings of 23 Kashmiri Hindus in the town of Wandhama in the Ganderbal District of Jammu and Kashmir, India on 25 January, 1998. The massacre was carried out by the militant outfits Lashkar-e-Taiba and Hizbul Mujahideen.

topPlaces.name: Devepora
topPlaces.description: Devepora (also known as Dev Pora or Devpura) is a large village located in the forest block of Shopian district in the Indian union territory of Jammu and Kashmir. The village is located in hill area of district Shopian which is 6 kilometers away from its town.

topPlaces.name: Nawabagh Ganderbal
topPlaces.description: Nawabagh is the notified area and village in Ganderbal district in the Indian union territory of Jammu and Kashmir. The place is famous for the production of apples, cherries, peaches, apricots, walnuts, and pears, which are exported all over India.

topPlaces.name: Zazun
topPlaces.description: Zazna is a village in Ganderbal district of Jammu and Kashmir, India. The village produces several varieties of apples, including 'Delicious' and 'American'.

topPlaces.name: Gogjigund
topPlaces.description: Gogjigund is a village and a Panchayat Halqa in Sherpathri Block of Ganderbal district in the union territory of Jammu and Kashmir, India. Most of the area in this village is under woods.

topPlaces.name: Wakura
topPlaces.description: Wakura (sometimes spelled Wakoora or Wakora) is a village located in the Ganderbal district of Jammu and Kashmir, India. In 2013 it was given tehsil status, with the tehsil headquarters located in Lar, 13 km away.

topPlaces.name: Beehama
topPlaces.description: Beehama is a village located in the central area of district Ganderbal, Jammu and Kashmir. It is situated 18 km (11 mi) from Srinagar.

topPlaces.name: Ganderbal
topPlaces.description: Ganderbal (Urdu: [ˈɡaːndəɾbəl], Kashmiri: [ˈɡaːn̪d̪arbal]) is a city and the headquarters of Ganderbal district in the Indian-administered union territory of Jammu and Kashmir, in disputed Kashmir region. Ganderbal, known for its natural environment and scenery, has tourist sites like Sonamarg and Manasbal Lake, and the annual Amarnath Yatra pilgrimage also traverses through Ganderbal district.

items.note: The 1998 Wandhama massacre refers to the killings of 23 Kashmiri Hindus in the town of Wandhama in the Ganderbal District of Jammu and Kashmir, India on 25 January, 1998. The massacre was carried out by the militant outfits Lashkar-e-Taiba and Hizbul Mujahideen.

Devepora (also known as Dev Pora or Devpura) is a large village located in the forest block of Shopian district in the Indian union territory of Jammu and Kashmir. The village is located in hill area of district Shopian which is 6 kilometers away from its town.

Nawabagh is the notified area and village in Ganderbal district in the Indian union territory of Jammu and Kashmir. The place is famous for the production of apples, cherries, peaches, apricots, walnuts, and pears, which are exported all over India.
Zazna is a village in Ganderbal district of Jammu and Kashmir, India. The village produces several varieties of apples, including 'Delicious' and 'American'.
Gogjigund is a village and a Panchayat Halqa in Sherpathri Block of Ganderbal district in the union territory of Jammu and Kashmir, India. Most of the area in this village is under woods.

Wakura (sometimes spelled Wakoora or Wakora) is a village located in the Ganderbal district of Jammu and Kashmir, India. In 2013 it was given tehsil status, with the tehsil headquarters located in Lar, 13 km away.
Beehama is a village located in the central area of district Ganderbal, Jammu and Kashmir. It is situated 18 km (11 mi) from Srinagar.

Ganderbal (Urdu: [ˈɡaːndəɾbəl], Kashmiri: [ˈɡaːn̪d̪arbal]) is a city and the headquarters of Ganderbal district in the Indian-administered union territory of Jammu and Kashmir, in disputed Kashmir region. Ganderbal, known for its natural environment and scenery, has tourist sites like Sonamarg and Manasbal Lake, and the annual Amarnath Yatra pilgrimage also traverses through Ganderbal district.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kheer Bhawani?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kheer Bhawani is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kheer Bhawani?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Sheikh ul-Alam International Airport, Srinagar (26 km) and the nearest railway station is Katra (Vaishno Devi railhead) (139 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kheer Bhawani?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kheer Bhawani at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kheer Bhawani?
faq.a: Stays in Kheer Bhawani start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kheer Bhawani?
faq.a: The top attractions are 1998 Wandhama massacre, Devepora, Nawabagh Ganderbal, Zazun, Gogjigund.
